title: AI can use digital tools and external systems reliably
description: An AI system can choose tools well, execute across systems, recover from failures, and use documentation to operate unfamiliar interfaces.

subQuestions.title: Can it choose the right tool for the task?
subQuestions.description: Measures whether the system selects appropriate tools instead of defaulting to text-only reasoning or the wrong interface.
subQuestions.rationale: Tool selection is now strong in many bounded environments where available tools and permissions are explicit.

proofItems.title: Benchmark suite: Tool selection in bounded agent environments
proofItems.shortExplanation: Tool-use benchmarks show high rates of correct tool choice when the task and available interfaces are clearly defined.

subQuestions.title: Can it execute multi-tool workflows correctly?
subQuestions.description: Tests whether the system can carry state and intent across multiple connected applications and services.
subQuestions.rationale: Cross-system execution is increasingly practical, but brittle handoffs and partial completion still limit reliability.

proofItems.title: Deployment audit: Cross-tool workflow execution
proofItems.shortExplanation: Operational audits show good performance on bounded multi-app flows, but recurring failures when errors propagate across tools.

subQuestions.title: Can it recover from tool or API failures?
subQuestions.description: Measures whether the system retries, reroutes, or escalates sensibly after external failures rather than silently stalling.
subQuestions.rationale: Recovery patterns exist, but robust failure handling remains one of the main gaps between demos and production-grade autonomy.

proofItems.title: Deployment audit: Tool and API failure recovery
proofItems.shortExplanation: Agent traces show partial recovery behavior, but still frequent stalls and incorrect retries after external system failures.

subQuestions.title: Can it learn new tools from documentation?
subQuestions.description: Tests whether the system can read docs, infer usage patterns, and apply them correctly in a live task.
subQuestions.rationale: Documentation-grounded tool use is already one of the better developed areas of practical agent performance.

proofItems.title: Leaderboard: Documentation-grounded tool adoption
proofItems.shortExplanation: Leaderboard results show top systems learning unfamiliar APIs and tool flows directly from documentation with modest correction.

subQuestions.title: Can it leave an auditable trail of actions taken?
subQuestions.description: Measures whether external actions, intermediate states, and decisions can be reconstructed after the fact.
subQuestions.rationale: Action logging is increasingly feasible, but trace quality and completeness still vary a lot across agent setups.

proofItems.title: Deployment audit: Auditable action traces for agent workflows
proofItems.shortExplanation: Trace audits show improving visibility into tool actions, though missing state transitions still limit full reconstruction.
